summary: I'm Jamie—a curious, people-loving (97% extrovert!) Human Resources professional who thrives on solving problems through data analysis, developing people programs grounded in evidence, and collaborating with stakeholders for sustainable impact. Expect me to always be seeking ways we can improve the people experience—through human-centered, evidence-based approaches.

bullets.text: Upskill HR team in {data|data analytics} to audit 300+ leave cases, successfully refuting cost assumptions via {data|evidence-based insights}
Empower HR to present executive-ready insights on $362K in leave costs, leading to policy redesigns and first aid training
Conduct focus groups and 1:1 interviews with an NGO leadership team to surface decision-making and accountability gaps
Develop guides and workflows for 70+ {cross|cross-functional} staff, improving program knowledge and reducing onboarding time by 75%
Facilitate training webinars for 600+ participants, enhancing {prog|program understanding} and driving a 78% program enrollment rate
Conduct needs assessments with 25+ global {cross|stakeholders} to identify learning gaps and design 3 new educational programs
Manage 20+ programs and 10+ vendors, refining program positioning, delivery strategy, and {coord|communications strategy} via {data|feedback analysis}
Improved {ex|employee experience} via 5 {cross|cross-functional} projects focusing on rewards & recognition, survey development, and L&D
Led a manager effectiveness initiative, transforming {data|feedback data} into actionable reports that informed engagement strategies
Analyzed 2,000+ employee experiences to amplify employee voice and guide C-suite decisions on satisfaction drivers
Provided consultations to improve strategic plans and internal training on DEI, Inclusive Leadership, and performance review
Strengthened {ex|engagement} via an HR {coord|newsletter} and award-winning well-being initiatives, recognized among 1,000+ competitors
Optimized {coord|onboarding processes} for new hires across 3 global locations by conducting process analysis and clarifying job responsibilities
Initiated a DEI pilot with an Inclusive Leadership workshop for 12 senior leaders, later expanded to 23 other global locations
Facilitated smooth organizational change by directing a Mergers & Acquisitions project, overseeing {cross|cross-functional stakeholders}
Developed proficiency across various HR functions, ranging from recruitment and compensation to performance management
Filled a challenging international role by drafting job descriptions, posting openings, and researching global compensation details
Engaged global talent via personalized communication and customer service, increasing applications by 566% in 2 months
Organized campus recruiting events and moderated panels with C-suite executives at top universities to attract talent

changeLog.note: Tailored for Oberto Snacks HR Coordinator (Kent WA, on-site CPG plant). Role-type = HR/HRBP. Summary identity -> 'Human Resources' professional + 'developing people programs grounded in evidence' (3 pillars kept, fixed tail). InGenius = Set H (establish cross-functional processes + documentation, listening sessions/needs assessments, communications strategy, program enrollment rate) to map to JD's internal-communications + new-hire enablement + HR-program coordination duties. Vestas HR-variant leads with HR newsletter + award-winning well-being engagement (= internal communications + recognition programs) and onboarding-for-new-hires (kept phrase 'new hires'); added ER-cases-with-confidentiality bullet (maps to confidential-info + general HR support). NextGen 4 real bullets (employee-experience first). Kronos 4 real (recruitment/comp, JD-drafting/posting). ODN Pair A (Upskill/Empower, completed quantified results). Skills: surfaced ADP first in HRIS (JD names ADP explicitly) + MS Office (Word/PowerPoint) JD-named foundational. Header = Kent, WA (city-based on-site role). No invented tools/numbers; canonical skills vocabulary only; ODN full name; Wesleyan Coursework kept.
